behaviors.description: The generated function signature matches the expected interface (parameter names, types, return type) specified in the description.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the model’s interpretation of natural language into code shape; stubs can only replay correct/incorrect signatures and miss real misinterpretation drift. REAL-INFRA is not required, but LLM-DEP is explicit because correctness is about generated content. Rejected Workflow: behavior text targets a single generation outcome (signature match), not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run the service against real LLM generation for several interface-constrained prompts; parse the first generated function signature and assert exact parameter names/types/return type match expected interface.

behaviors.description: Orchestration executes attempts in order and returns immediately on the first passing attempt.
behaviors.justification: This is module handoff/state-machine behavior (attempt runner + test executor + stop condition). INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess required by behavior text, (b) no dependence on model output quality, (c) no multi-agentic requirement, (d) orchestration here is deterministic and can be validated with stubs. Rejected Agentic via text: "executes attempts in order and returns immediately" asserts control flow, not model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Wire orchestrator with deterministic stubs: attempt1 fails, attempt2 passes, attempt3 would fail if called. Assert call order [1,2], immediate return after pass, and attempt3 not executed.

behaviors.description: For a function that a single call usually fails, the live iterative loop can still produce a passing implementation within the allowed attempts.
behaviors.justification: Requires multiple real agentic steps (initial generation, failure-informed refinement(s), retest) and success depends on model outputs across steps. A mock would hide whether iterative improvement actually emerges. Rejected Agentic: behavior text explicitly requires the "live iterative loop" and success "within allowed attempts," i.e., sequenced agentic decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Use a known prompt where first attempt commonly fails; run real loop end-to-end and assert at least one run achieves pass by attempt <=3, with recorded failing early attempt and later passing attempt.

behaviors.description: When tests fail, the refinement prompt builder includes the failed test names and failure messages in the next LLM prompt.
behaviors.justification: Real failure is often at the boundary between test-result schema and prompt-construction module (field loss/mismatch), not model quality. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess required, (b) assertion is prompt content inclusion, not AI output correctness, (c) no multi-agentic chain needed. Rejected Agentic via text: "includes ... in the next LLM prompt" focuses on payload construction only.
behaviors.test_description: Run failed test results through real test-result serializer + prompt builder wiring, then assert resulting refinement prompt contains each failed test name and failure message verbatim/normalized as specified.

behaviors.description: The next live attempt changes implementation content in response to prior failure context rather than resubmitting the same code.
behaviors.justification: Needs at least two agentic generations plus failure-context handoff; correctness depends on model reacting to prior failures. Deterministic stubs can force differences but cannot validate real adaptive behavior. Rejected Agentic: behavior text requires "next live attempt" responding to "prior failure context," which is sequential multi-step agentic flow.
behaviors.test_description: Execute live generate->test(fail)->refine->generate cycle; compare attempt N and N+1 code bodies and assert substantive change aligned with failure context (not identical/resubmission).

behaviors.description: The service stops after 3 failed attempts and returns a structured error with all three attempts and their test results.
behaviors.justification: Primary risk is orchestration/bookkeeping across modules (attempt counter, result aggregation, error shaping). INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess needed by behavior text, (b) no dependence on model output quality, (c) no multi-agentic decision quality requirement. Rejected System via text: behavior is about stopping/structuring after "3 failed attempts," not external infra behavior.
behaviors.test_description: With deterministic failing generator/test stubs, run loop and assert exactly 3 attempts executed, termination occurs, and returned error payload contains all three attempt artifacts and test outcomes in order.

behaviors.description: The full non-agentic pipeline wiring (generate call, test execution, attempt bookkeeping) works end to end with deterministic stubbed model outputs.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly scopes to "non-agentic" and "deterministic stubbed model outputs," so objective is local module wiring end-to-end. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no required real OS/network/subprocess stated, (b) model-output quality explicitly excluded, (c) no required multi-agentic dependence. Rejected System using text: "deterministic stubbed model outputs" and non-agentic wiring indicate in-process composition suffices.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end pipeline in-process with stub model outputs and controlled test runner outcomes; assert generate invocation, test execution, attempt records, and final status all wire correctly.

behaviors.description: The full live loop (generate, test, refine, retest) solves at least one known hard function that fails in the first attempt.
behaviors.justification: This is explicitly a sequenced live agent loop with multiple model-dependent decisions and step-to-step adaptation. Mocking would hide true capability to recover from first-attempt failure. Rejected Agentic: behavior text includes full chain "generate, test, refine, retest" and requirement that first attempt fails then later succeeds.
behaviors.test_description: Run real loop on a curated hard prompt where attempt 1 is expected to fail; assert recorded first failure and subsequent pass within max attempts, capturing intermediate artifacts for traceability.
